ownershipRule: Each capability and event has exactly one owner: go-api, desktop-bridge, temporary-ipc, or deprecated.
knownCompatibilitySurfaces: reorderPlans is exposed by preload and handled by main, but is absent from AutoplanApi and has no renderer caller.
runTaskBatches is exposed by preload and handled by main; WorkspacePage uses an explicit local type extension because AutoplanApi does not declare it.
updates:status is intentionally both an invoke channel and a main-to-renderer event; command and event ownership are separate rows.

capabilities.module: Electron app lifecycle (currently only embedded in UpdateStatus)

capabilities.sideEffects: spawn, health-check and terminate the localhost Go sidecar
capabilities.fallback: disable Go feature flags; no current sidecar process

capabilities.sideEffects: stop runtime/terminals and delete project-owned rows
open a native file manager window
open a native terminal in the project directory
create and open the persistent AutoPlan log directory
capabilities.fallback: open the AppData AutoPlan logs directory manually

capabilities.sideEffects: validate path then open file or containing folder
persist runtime/MCP settings and possibly restart MCP
start scheduler/runtime and emit loop events
stop project runtime and emit loop events
read secret setting; must not enter logs/events
persist MCP settings and schedule restart
read plan file under authorized workspace
append task to linked intake plan and persist plan
delete plan/tasks and associated plan file as configured
spawn task execution and persist operation state
terminate task child process and persist terminal state
reset acceptance and append supplement/requeue work
insert requirement, attachments and plan-generation state
update requirement and replace attachment metadata/files
delete requirement, links, attachments and eligible plan data
insert feedback, attachments and plan-generation state
update feedback/link and replace attachment metadata/files
delete feedback, links, attachments and eligible plan data
append task and rewrite linked plan
spawn planner and persist generated plan/tasks
spawn configured script process and capture output
spawn executor process and persist operation
read selected JSON and import executor definitions
spawn PTY in validated project workspace
close and remove retained terminal session
network request and controlled installer download
persist dismissed version and emit status
persist auto-check setting and emit status
open URL in default browser after scheme validation
persist/queue message and start AI/CLI process
clear controller history and delete chat_messages
persist legacy/global AI config and emit ai-config changed
remove queued item and persist queue
update queued message and persist queue
persist encrypted/controlled credential and emit changed
update credential/config, invalidate controllers, emit changed
delete config, invalidate controllers, emit changed
persist controlled auth token and emit changed
persist singleton default and emit changed
persist scope/cross-project/allowed roots; warn on all

events.ordering: A full snapshot supersedes every queued patch; useSnapshot clears queued patches and commits at the next animation frame.
Patches are coalesced by project per animation frame; a preceding full snapshot is applied first and a later full snapshot discards queued patches.
Each push is a complete latest status; renderer replaces prior status in arrival order.

events.producer: TerminalService DATA -> terminalIpc safeTerminalEvent -> main window
events.ordering: Per-session byte chunks preserve PTY emission order and precede the corresponding exit/closed terminal event.

events.producer: TerminalService EXIT -> terminalIpc safeTerminalEvent -> main window
events.ordering: Terminal output already emitted remains ordered before exit; exit is terminal for the PTY but retained sessions may later be closed.

events.producer: TerminalService STATUS -> terminalIpc safeTerminalEvent -> main window
events.ordering: Status transitions are applied in emission order for a session; later status replaces earlier state.

events.producer: TerminalService CLOSED -> terminalIpc safeTerminalEvent -> main window
events.ordering: Closed is the final lifecycle notification and removes the session after any exit/status event.
Chunks preserve controller emission order per conversation and must arrive before chat:done for that run.
Exactly one terminal event follows the final chunk for a run; queued work may start only after the prior run terminal event.
Each event is a complete queue snapshot; renderer replaces the prior queue only for the active conversation.
Mutation commits before the complete config-list event; later events replace earlier config state.
Mutation commits before the complete config-list event; later events replace earlier config state.
